generatedSentence: The city pulsed with anarchy and traffic, its fracked foundations threatening to undermine mankind. A worthless thumbnail of hope drifting whenever a scandal broke, judges often voided justice. Our tracker, known only by his brand, felt an itchier need to ransack the corrupt payroll records. The kitschy trophy in his wallets was a marginal reminder of what he fought for, an onboard gateway to uncover their treasures. His logbook, detailing a mammoth payroll heist, was filled with bookish notes, sometimes streaky from rain, other times smelling faintly of rhubarb. He knew the redskin cavalry, often paunchy and stuffy, would try to maintain their hold. He saw the effect of their corruption in the vacant eyes of a toddler, an orphan whose toys were just earthworms. The tracker felt a jumpy, headlong urgency, knowing that one day the payback would come for all kinds. The eclipse signaled chaos; a bacillus of madness was loose, making everyone itchier. He knew this was the episode to make superiors pay, a pivotal volt in the long tryout against them. A hunchback ally, skilled in widdershins swordplay, joined him, a true warrior amongst bricklayers. They moved like shadows, near the edge of a new dimension, ready to uncover the diamonds hidden by the milkman syndicate, their mission bordering on euphoria. Our heroes, a league of parkour specialists and cowgirls, leaped across rooftops, their laughter echoing through the northern district. With a marvelous yell, they found the magnet, a beacon to the criminals' illicit drachma. There was no nicety in their approach, only a frothy madness as they saw the last gallon of gunky treacle being poured into the voided accounts. Their ailurophile leader, adorned with an orchid necktie, watched as the kittens, symbols of the corrupt, were finally brought to justice, their claws no match for the upbeat warriors. Gotcha!
panelSentences: The city pulsed with anarchy and traffic, its fracked foundations threatening to undermine mankind. The kitschy trophy in his wallets was a marginal reminder of what he fought for, an onboard gateway to uncover their treasures.

His logbook, detailing a mammoth payroll heist, was filled with bookish notes, sometimes streaky from rain, other times smelling faintly of rhubarb. He knew the redskin cavalry, often paunchy and stuffy, would try to maintain their hold. He saw the effect of their corruption in the vacant eyes of a toddler, an orphan whose toys were just earthworms. The tracker felt a jumpy, headlong urgency, knowing that one day the payback would come for all kinds.

The eclipse signaled chaos; a bacillus of madness was loose, making everyone itchier. He knew this was the episode to make superiors pay, a pivotal volt in the long tryout against them. A hunchback ally, skilled in widdershins swordplay, joined him, a true warrior amongst bricklayers. They moved like shadows, near the edge of a new dimension, ready to uncover the diamonds hidden by the milkman syndicate, their mission bordering on euphoria.

Our heroes, a league of parkour specialists and cowgirls, leaped across rooftops, their laughter echoing through the northern district. With a marvelous yell, they found the magnet, a beacon to the criminals' illicit drachma. There was no nicety in their approach, only a frothy madness as they saw the last gallon of gunky treacle being poured into the voided accounts. Their ailurophile leader, adorned with an orchid necktie, watched as the kittens, symbols of the corrupt, were finally brought to justice, their claws no match for the upbeat warriors. Gotcha!
rawPanelSentences: The city pulsed with anarchy and traffic, its fracked foundations threatening to undermine mankind. A worthless thumbnail of hope drifting whenever a scandal broke, judges often voided justice. Our tracker, known only by his brand, felt an itchier need to ransack the corrupt payroll records. The kitschy trophy in his wallets was a marginal reminder of what he fought for, an onboard gateway to uncover their treasures.

His logbook, detailing a mammoth payroll heist, was filled with bookish notes, sometimes streaky from rain, other times smelling faintly of rhubarb. He knew the redskin cavalry, often paunchy and stuffy, would try to maintain their hold. He saw the effect of their corruption in the vacant eyes of a toddler, an orphan whose toys were just earthworms. The tracker felt a jumpy, headlong urgency, knowing that one day the payback would come for all kinds.

The eclipse signaled chaos; a bacillus of madness was loose, making everyone itchier. He knew this was the episode to make superiors pay, a pivotal volt in the long tryout against them. A hunchback ally, skilled in widdershins swordplay, joined him, a true warrior amongst bricklayers. They moved like shadows, near the edge of a new dimension, ready to uncover the diamonds hidden by the milkman syndicate, their mission bordering on euphoria.

Our heroes, a league of parkour specialists and cowgirls, leaped across rooftops, their laughter echoing through the northern district. With a marvelous yell, they found the magnet, a beacon to the criminals' illicit drachma. There was no nicety in their approach, only a frothy madness as they saw the last gallon of gunky treacle being poured into the voided accounts. Their ailurophile leader, adorned with an orchid necktie, watched as the kittens, symbols of the corrupt, were finally brought to justice, their claws no match for the upbeat warriors. Gotcha!
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean, agile figure wearing a dark tech-suit with glowing blue accents and a silver visor. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist crouched on a rooftop overlooking a dystopian city skyline at twilight, observing chaotic traffic below, wide shot from a slightly elevated angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist in a tense standoff with a group of paunchy, uniformed guards (redskin cavalry) in a lavish, dilapidated office, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist and a hunchback ally engaged in dynamic widdershins swordplay against shadowy figures in a dark, neon-lit alleyway, low angle action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist and a diverse team (cowgirls, parkour specialists) celebrating with triumphant laughter around a glowing magnet, with kittens playing playfully nearby, eye-level medium shot.

definitions.definition: Having no worth or use; without value; inconsequential.
definitions.example: Don't be a worthless slouch! Go get yourself a job.

definitions.definition: Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.
Eager search.
To loot or pillage. See also sack.
To make a vigorous and thorough search of (a place, person) with a view to stealing something, especially when leaving behind a state of disarray.
definitions.example: to ransack a house for valuables

definitions.definition: To examine carefully; to investigate.
To violate; to ravish; to deflower.
The recoil of an object bouncing off another.
A return to health or well-being; a recovery.
definitions.example: I am on the rebound.

definitions.definition: An effort to recover from a setback.
A romantic partner with whom one begins a relationship (or the relationship one begins) for the sake of getting over a previous, recently-ended romantic relationship.
The strike of the ball after it has bounced off a defending player, the crossbar or goalpost.
An instance of catching the ball after it has hit the rim or backboard without a basket being scored, generally credited to a particular player.
To bound or spring back from a force.
To give back an echo.
To jump up or get back up again.
To send back; to reverberate.
The state of a society being without authorities or an authoritative governing body.
Anarchism; the political theory that a community is best organized by the voluntary cooperation of individuals, rather than by a government, which is regarded as being coercive by nature.
A chaotic and confusing absence of any form of political authority or government.
Confusion in general; disorder.
A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.
A person who decides the fate of someone or something that has been called into question.
A person officiating at a sports event or similar.
definitions.example: At a boxing match, the decision of the judges is final.

definitions.definition: A person who evaluates something or forms an opinion.
definitions.example: She is a good judge of wine.

definitions.definition: To sit in judgment on; to pass sentence on.
definitions.example: A higher power will judge you after you are dead.

definitions.definition: To sit in judgment, to act as judge.
definitions.example: Justices in this country judge without appeal.

definitions.definition: To form an opinion on.
definitions.example: I judge a man’s character by the cut of his suit.

definitions.definition: To arbitrate; to pass opinion on something, especially to settle a dispute etc.
definitions.example: We cannot both be right: you must judge between us.

definitions.definition: To have as an opinion; to consider, suppose.
definitions.example: I judge it safe to leave the house once again.

definitions.definition: To form an opinion; to infer.
definitions.example: I judge from the sky that it might rain later.

definitions.definition: To criticize or label another person or thing.
To move slowly, especially pushed by currents of water, air, etc.
definitions.example: The balloon was drifting in the breeze.

definitions.definition: To move haphazardly without any destination.
definitions.example: He drifted from town to town, never settling down.

definitions.definition: To deviate gently from the intended direction of travel.
definitions.example: This car tends to drift left at high speeds.

definitions.definition: To drive or carry, as currents do a floating body.
To drive into heaps.
definitions.example: A current of wind drifts snow or sand

definitions.definition: To accumulate in heaps by the force of wind; to be driven into heaps.
definitions.example: Snow or sand drifts.

definitions.definition: To make a drift; to examine a vein or ledge for the purpose of ascertaining the presence of metals or ores; to follow a vein; to prospect.
To enlarge or shape, as a hole, with a drift.
To oversteer a vehicle, causing loss of traction, while maintaining control from entry to exit of a corner. See Drifting (motorsport).
The act by which something drifts.
That which drifts.
A driving technique where the driver intentionally oversteers, causing loss of traction in the rear wheels, while maintaining control from entry to exit of a corner.
Moving aimlessly or at the mercy of external forces.
definitions.example: The drifting seaweed went wherever the currents carried it.

definitions.definition: Without direction, focus, or goal.
The military arm of service that fights while riding horses.
An individual unit of the cavalry arm of service.
The branch of the military transported by fast light vehicles, also known as mechanized cavalry.
An entrance capable of being blocked by use of a gate.
Any point that represents the beginning of a transition from one place or phase to another.
A point at which freight moving from one territory to another is interchanged between transportation lines.

(digital communications) In wireless internet, an access point with additional software capabilities such as providing NAT and DHCP, which may also provide VPN support, roaming, firewalls, various levels of security, etc.
(digital communications) To make available via a gateway, or access point.
Agent noun of track; one who, or that which, tracks or pursues, as a man or dog that follows game.
In an organ, a light strip of wood connecting (in path) a key and a pallet, to communicate motion by pulling.
A type of computer software for composing music by aligning samples on parallel timelines.
A musician who writes music in a tracker.
A computer program that monitors something.
A tracker mortgage.
A list of employees who receive salary or wages, together with the amounts due to each.
The total sum of money paid to employees.
The calculation of salaries and wages and the deduction of taxes etc.; the department in a company responsible for this.
Bribes paid to people
To place on a payroll.
Having the nature of kitsch.
A small case, often flat and often made of leather, for keeping money (especially paper money), credit cards, etc.
definitions.example: The thief stole all the money and credit cards out of the old man's wallet.

definitions.definition: (by extension) A person's bank account or assets.
definitions.example: It's unknown if the pro running back's recent sex scandal will hit him in the wallet or not.

definitions.definition: A thick case or folder with plastic sleeves in which compact discs may be stored.
definitions.example: I won an auction online for a cheap CD wallet.

definitions.definition: A bag or pouch.
definitions.example: He brought with him a large wallet with some provisions for the road.

definitions.definition: A person's buttocks (the area of the body nearest where one keeps one's wallet).
definitions.example: He fell down and landed on his wallet.

definitions.definition: (interrogative) When ever: emphatic form of when.
definitions.example: Whenever will I find time to finish that jigsaw?

definitions.definition: At any time.
definitions.example: Feel free to visit whenever.

definitions.definition: At any time that.
definitions.example: Visit whenever you want to.

definitions.definition: At the (single) time that, no matter when.
definitions.example: Tomorrow I'll get up whenever the sun rises.

definitions.definition: Every time that.
definitions.example: Whenever he has a pair of aces, his eyelids twitch.

definitions.definition: Regardless of the time that.
definitions.example: Income must be reported in the period in which it is earned, whenever payment is actually received.

definitions.definition: When.
definitions.example: Whenever I was a child, I lived in Arkansas.

definitions.definition: To make invalid or worthless.
definitions.example: He voided the check and returned it.

definitions.definition: To empty.
definitions.example: void one’s bowels

definitions.definition: To throw or send out; to evacuate; to emit; to discharge.
definitions.example: to void excrement

definitions.definition: To withdraw, depart.
To remove the contents of; to make or leave vacant or empty; to quit; to leave.
definitions.example: to void a table

definitions.definition: With the centre cut out.
An incident or event that disgraces or damages the reputation of the persons or organization involved.
definitions.example: Their affair was reported as a scandal by most tabloids.

definitions.definition: Damage to one's reputation.
definitions.example: The incident brought considerable scandal to his family.

definitions.definition: Widespread moral outrage, indignation, as over an offence to decency.
definitions.example: When their behaviour was made public it caused a great scandal.

definitions.definition: Religious discredit; an act or behaviour which brings a religion into discredit.
Something which hinders acceptance of religious ideas or behaviour; a stumbling-block or offense.
Defamatory talk; gossip, slander.
definitions.example: According to village scandal, they weren't even married.

definitions.definition: To treat opprobriously; to defame; to slander.
To scandalize; to offend.
A worm that lives in the ground.
A worm of the family Lumbricidae, or, more generally, of the suborder Lumbricina.
A contemptible person; a groveller.
An excited state of joy; a feeling of intense happiness.
definitions.example: The runner was in absolute euphoria after winning his first marathon.

definitions.definition: To become a part of a group; to incorporate (someone) into a group.
To begin to use a product or service; to take (someone) on as a new customer of a product or service.
Carried or used on or in a vehicle or vessel
(by extension) Being a part of, being included in, participating in
definitions.example: James is onboard as a production manager.

definitions.definition: On or in a vehicle or vessel; aboard; on board.
To dig underneath (something), to make a passage for destructive or military purposes; to sap.
To weaken or work against; to hinder, sabotage.
definitions.example: The war efforts were undermined by the constant bickering between the allies.

definitions.definition: To erode the base or foundation of something, e.g. by the action of water.
To employ hydraulic fracturing (fracking)
Fuck.
To make greatly distressed and/or a discomposed appearance
To be placed or place someone under the influence of a psychedelic drug
To streak; to variegate
To experience reality withdrawal, or hallucinations (nightmarish), to behave irrational or unconventional due to drug use.
To react extremely or irrationally, usually under distress or discomposure
A streak or variegation in a pattern.
(minced oath) Fucking.
definitions.example: You're getting on my freaking nerves!

definitions.definition: Freakish.
Fucking.
definitions.example: He was so scared he freaking ran into a police station.

definitions.definition: Any fish of the order Siluriformes, mainly found in fresh water, lacking scales, and having barbels like whiskers around the mouth
To fish for catfish
definitions.example: I only use this rod for catfishing.

definitions.definition: A conflagration; a flame.
A piece of burning wood or peat, or a glowing cinder.
definitions.example: To burn something to brands and ashes.

definitions.definition: A torch used for signaling.
A sword.
A mark or scar made by burning with a hot iron, especially to mark cattle or to classify the contents of a cask.
A branding iron.
The symbolic identity, represented by a name and/or a logo, which indicates a certain product or service to the public.
A specific product, service, or provider so distinguished.
definitions.example: Some brands of breakfast cereal contain a lot of sugar.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any specific type or variety of something; a distinct style or manner.
definitions.example: I didn't appreciate his particular brand of flattery.

definitions.definition: The public image or reputation and recognized, typical style of an individual or group.
A mark of infamy; stigma.
Any minute fungus producing a burnt appearance in plants.
To burn the flesh with a hot iron, either as a marker (for criminals, slaves etc.) or to cauterise a wound.
definitions.example: When they caught him, he was branded and then locked up.

definitions.definition: To mark (especially cattle) with a brand as proof of ownership.
definitions.example: The ranch hands had to brand every new calf by lunchtime.

definitions.definition: To make an indelible impression on the memory or senses.
definitions.example: Her face is branded upon my memory.

definitions.definition: To stigmatize, label (someone).
definitions.example: He was branded a fool by everyone that heard his story.

definitions.definition: To associate a product or service with a trademark or other name and related images.
definitions.example: They branded the new detergent "Suds-O", with a nature scene inside a green O on the muted-colored recycled-cardboard box.

definitions.definition: The fingernail on the thumb.
A rough sketch (e.g., the size of one's thumbnail).
A small picture, used as a compact representation of a larger image.
To describe concisely.
To create a smaller representation of (a larger image).
Pedestrians or vehicles on roads, or the flux or passage thereof.
definitions.example: The traffic is slow during rush hour.

definitions.definition: Commercial transportation or exchange of goods, or the movement of passengers or people.
Illegal trade or exchange of goods, often drugs.
Exchange or flux of information, messages or data, as in a computer or telephone network.
Commodities of the market.
To pass goods and commodities from one person to another for an equivalent in goods or money; to buy or sell goods
To trade meanly or mercenarily; to bargain.
To exchange in traffic; to effect by a bargain or for a consideration.
A small detail or distinction.
definitions.example: We met the new captain while we were taking enemy fire and were unable to observe the niceties of formal introductions.

definitions.definition: Subtlety or precision of use.
definitions.example: A rocket-propelled grenade doesn't have the nicety of a sniper round, but you must admit its effectiveness.

definitions.definition: Delicacy of character or feeling usually from excessive refinement; fastidiousness
That which is delicate to the taste.
The state of being mad; insanity; mental disease.
Rash folly
The human race in its entirety.
Men collectively, as opposed to all women.
Human feelings; humanity.
Something that is marginal.
A constituency won with a small margin.
Of, relating to, or located at or near a margin or edge; also figurative usages of location and margin (edge).
definitions.example: In recent years there has been an increase in violence against marginal groups.

definitions.definition: Determined by a small margin; having a salient characteristic determined by a small margin.
Pertaining to changes resulting from a unit increase in production or consumption of a good.
An alignment of astronomical objects whereby one object comes between the observer (or notional observer) and another object, thus obscuring the latter.

Especially, an alignment whereby a planetary object (for example, the Moon) comes between the Sun and another planetary object (for example, the Earth), resulting in a shadow being cast by the middle planetary object onto the other planetary object.
A seasonal state of plumage in some birds, notably ducks, adopted temporarily after the breeding season and characterised by a dull and scruffy appearance.
Obscurity, decline, downfall
Of astronomical bodies, to cause an eclipse.
definitions.example: The Moon eclipsed the Sun.

definitions.definition: To overshadow; to be better or more noticeable than.
(Irish grammar) To undergo eclipsis.
A reflected sound that is heard again by its initial observer.
An utterance repeating what has just been said.
A device in verse in which a line ends with a word which recalls the sound of the last word of the preceding line.
Sympathetic recognition; response; answer.
The displaying on the command line of the command that has just been executed.
The letter E in the ICAO spelling alphabet.

(whist) A signal, played in the same manner as a trump signal, made by a player who holds four or more trumps (or, as played by some, exactly three trumps) and whose partner has led trumps or signalled for trumps.
(whist) A signal showing the number held of a plain suit when a high card in that suit is led by one's partner.
(of a sound or sound waves) To reflect off a surface and return.
To reflect back (a sound).
(by extension) To repeat (another's speech, opinion etc.).
definitions.example: Sid echoed his father's point of view.

definitions.definition: To repeat its input as input to some other device or system.
(whist) To give the echo signal, informing one's partner about cards one holds.
The visual image formed by an echocardiograph.
The use of ultrasound to produce images of the heart.
A person with ailurophilia; a cat-lover.
An unaccented beat at the start of a musical phrase.
definitions.example: The conductor said: I give you three beats for nothing and then you come in on the upbeat.

definitions.definition: Having a fast pace, tempo, or beat.
definitions.example: The notes are easy, but it's an upbeat tune and should be played fairly quickly.

definitions.definition: Having a positive, lively, or perky tone, attitude, etc.
definitions.example: He sounded upbeat when I talked to him.

definitions.definition: Greasy, messy or dirty.
Tropæum.
An object, usually in the form of a statuette, cup, or shield, awarded for success in a competition or to mark a special achievement.
definitions.example: He won the trophy in a running competition.

definitions.definition: An object taken as a prize by a hunter or conqueror, especially one that is displayed.
definitions.example: The set of antlers which hung on the wall was his prized trophy.

definitions.definition: Any emblem of success; a status symbol.
definitions.example: His trophies included his second wife, his successful children, the third and fourth homes in Palm Beach and Malibu, his three yachts (for the Pacific, the Atlantic, and the Mediterranean), his jet, and his mistresses.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object taken by a serial killer or rapist as a memento of the crime.

A display of weaponry and other militaria, often captured from a defeated enemy, as an ornament designed for the purpose of triumphalist display by a victor or as a show of military prowess by a monarch.
Full of kinks; liable to kink or curl.
definitions.example: kinky hair

definitions.definition: Marked by unconventional sexual preferences or behavior, as fetishism, sadomasochism, and other sexual practices.
Queer; eccentric; crotchety.
The wrong way.
Anticlockwise, counter-clockwise.
definitions.example: It is unlucky to walk widdershins around a church.

definitions.definition: A man who delivers milk to households and sometimes businesses early in the morning.
To cause irritation or deep bitterness.
To fester.
definitions.example: a splinter rankles in the flesh

definitions.definition: A book in which measurements from the ship's log are recorded, along with other salient details of the voyage.
(by extension) A book in which events are recorded; a journal, especially of travel.
A record of the ownership, and licensing of a motor car
An only child.
Alone in a category.
definitions.example: He is the only doctor for miles.

definitions.definition: Singularly superior; the best.
Without sibling; without a sibling of the same gender.
definitions.example: He is their only son, in fact, an only child.

definitions.definition: Mere.
Without others or anything further; exclusively.
definitions.example: My heart is hers, and hers only.

definitions.definition: No more than; just.
definitions.example: If there were only one more ticket!

definitions.definition: As recently as.
definitions.example: He left only moments ago.

definitions.definition: Used to express surprise or consternation at an action.
definitions.example: She's only gone and run off with the milkman!

definitions.definition: Introduces a disappointing or surprising outcome that renders futile something previously mentioned. See also only to, only for.
definitions.example: I helped him out only for him to betray me.

definitions.definition: Above all others; particularly.
Under the condition that; but.
definitions.example: You're welcome to borrow my bicycle, only please take care of it.

definitions.definition: But; except.
definitions.example: I would enjoy running, only I have this broken leg.

definitions.definition: One who is stooped or hunched over.
A deformed upper spinal column in the shape of a hump in the back.
A person with kyphosis, a spinal deformity that causes a hunched over appearance.
Somebody who possesses great bravery and carries out extraordinary or noble deeds.
A role model.
The protagonist in a work of fiction.
The current player, especially an hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: villain. Not to be confused with hero call.
definitions.example: Let's discuss how to play if the hero has KK, and there's an ace on board.

definitions.definition: A large sandwich made from meats and cheeses; a hero sandwich.
(food styling) The product chosen from several candidates to be photographed.
The eye-catching top portion of a web page, sometimes including a hero image; the portion above the fold.
Nervous and excited.
A glimmering glass-like mineral that is an allotrope of carbon in which each atom is surrounded by four others in the form of a tetrahedron.
definitions.example: The saw is coated with diamond.

definitions.definition: A gemstone made from this mineral.
definitions.example: The dozen loose diamonds sparkled in the light.

definitions.definition: A ring containing a diamond.
definitions.example: What a beautiful engagement diamond.

definitions.definition: A very pale blue color/colour.
Something that resembles a diamond.
A rhombus, especially when oriented so that its longer axis is vertical.
The polyiamond made up of two triangles.
The entire field of play used in the game.
The infield of a baseball field.
definitions.example: The teams met on the diamond.

definitions.definition: A card of the diamonds suit.
definitions.example: I have only one diamond in my hand.

definitions.definition: A size of type, standardised as 4 1/2 point.
To adorn with or as if with diamonds
One of the four suits of playing cards, marked with the symbol ♦.
The currency of Greece in ancient times and again from 1832 until 2001, with the symbol ₯, since replaced by the euro.
A coin worth one drachma.
An Ancient Greek weight of about 66.5 grains, or 4.3 grams.
A later Greek weight equal to a gram.
A flexible sac that can expand and contract and that holds liquids or gases.
Specifically, the urinary bladder.
A hollow, inflatable organ of a plant.
The inflatable bag inside various balls used in sports, such as footballs and rugby balls.
A sealed plastic bag that contains wine and is usually packaged in a cask.
Anything inflated, empty, or unsound.
To swell out like a bladder with air; to inflate.
To store or put up in bladders.
definitions.example: bladdered lard

definitions.definition: To fiddle with something in an attempt to fix, mend or improve it, especially in an experimental or unskilled manner.
To work as a tinker.
To tinker with; to tweak or attempt to fix.
An experimental fix or change.
A day of the week except those which form the weekend; that is:
Any day of the week (Monday through Sunday).

In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical potential and electromotive force (voltage); the potential difference across a conductor when a current of one ampere uses one watt of power. Symbol: V
Similar to sauce; having the consistency or texture of sauce.
Impertinent or disrespectful, often in a manner that is regarded as entertaining or amusing; smart.
definitions.example: She is a loud, saucy child who doesn't show a lot of respect to her elders.

definitions.definition: Impudently bold; pert.
Sharp; pungent; piquant.
Mildly erotic.
definitions.example: I enjoyed the dancing, but my wife found it a little too saucy.

definitions.definition: Fighting with a sword
definitions.example: His expertise at swordplay made his acting in the part of Hamlet very believable.

definitions.definition: The result or outcome of a cause.
definitions.example: The effect of the hurricane was a devastated landscape.

definitions.definition: Impression left on the mind; sensation produced.
Execution; performance; realization; operation.
An illusion produced by technical means (as in "special effect")
definitions.example: The effect of flying was most convincing.

definitions.definition: (sound engineering) An alteration, or device for producing an alteration, in sound after it has been produced by an instrument.
definitions.example: I just bought a couple of great effects.

definitions.definition: (etc.) A scientific phenomenon, usually named after its discoverer.
definitions.example: Doppler effect

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) Belongings, usually as personal effects.
Consequence intended; purpose; meaning; general intent; with to.
Reality; actual meaning; fact, as distinguished from mere appearance.
Manifestation; expression; sign.
To make or bring about; to implement.
definitions.example: The best way to effect change is to work with existing stakeholders.

definitions.definition: In any way.
Anyway, anyhow, in any case.
To precipitate.
Precipitous.
Plunging downwards head foremost.
Rushing forward without restraint.
Reckless, impetuous.
With the head first or down.
With an unrestrained forward motion.
definitions.example: Figures out today show the economy plunging headlong into recession.

definitions.definition: Rashly; precipitately; without deliberation, in haste, hastily
Fond of reading or studying, especially said of someone lacking social skills as a result.
Characterized by a method of expression generally found in books.
A craftsman who builds walls and suchlike out of bricks.
definitions.example: A good bricklayer should be able to calculate the number of bricks needed to build a wall.

definitions.definition: A potential problem or source of trouble.
definitions.example: Review the work thoroughly and make sure there are no gotchas.

definitions.definition: An instance of publicly tricking someone or exposing them to ridicule, especially by means of an elaborate deception.
An instance of accomplishing a tricky idea or overcoming a difficult obstacle.
definitions.example: Now here's another few gotchas that you can do to implement it.

definitions.definition: A feature of a system or a program that works in the way it is documented but is counter-intuitive and almost invites mistake or non-function.
definitions.example: Wireless was the first gotcha when installing the distro.

definitions.definition: A test of the suitability or effectiveness of a person or thing, especially of a performer or athlete (an audition)

Any species of the extinct genus Mammuthus, of large, usually hairy, elephant-like mammals with long curved tusks and an inclined back, which became extinct with the last retreat of ice age glaciers during the late Pleistocene period, and are known from fossils, frozen carcasses, and Paleolithic cave paintings found in North America and Eurasia.
A mastodon.
Something very large of its kind.
Comparable to a mammoth in its size; very large, huge, gigantic.
To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
To keep up; to preserve; to uphold (a state, condition etc.).
To declare or affirm (a clause) to be true; to assert.
A person of higher rank or quality.
The senior person in a monastic community.
The head of certain churches and colleges.
One who has made an original grant of heritable property to a tenant or vassal, on condition of a certain annual payment (feu duty) or of the performance of certain services.
Having streaks.
Used to describe a shot where the ball deflects off the edge of the bat, but is not caught by the slips or wicket-keeper and instead results in runs for the batsman.
(chiefly of a person, usually North America) Having alternating periods of good and bad performances; inconsistent.
definitions.example: Jones has always been a streaky hitter

definitions.definition: Not well-executed, due to luck more than skill.
(now sometimes considered an ethnic slur and offensive) An American Indian, a Native American.
Wire used to bind bales of hay.
Roughly-made, unsophisticated, decrepit (from the use of haywire for temporary repairs).
Behaving erratically or uncontrollably, especially of a machine or mechanical process; usually used with the verb "go".
definitions.example: It was working fine until it went haywire and wouldn't stop printing blank sheets.

definitions.definition: A spaceship engine that allows faster-than-light travel by entering hyperspace.
definitions.example: The ship's hyperdrive had failed, leaving them stranded years from any repair base.

definitions.definition: A state of accelerated action.
definitions.example: With enemies closing in fast, our hero's mind sprung into hyperdrive.

definitions.definition: A unit of volume, equivalent to eight pints
Exactly 4.54609 liters; an imperial gallon
231 cubic inches or approximately 3.785 liters for liquids (a "U.S. liquid gallon")
One-eighth of a U.S. bushel or approximately 4.405 liters for dry goods (a "U.S. dry gallon").
(in the plural) A large quantity (of any liquid).
definitions.example: The pipe burst and gallons of water flooded into the kitchen.

definitions.definition: A collection of valuable things; accumulated wealth; a stock of money, jewels, etc.
Anything greatly valued.
A term of endearment.
(of a person or thing) To consider to be precious; to value highly.
definitions.example: Oh, this ring is beautiful! I’ll treasure it forever.

definitions.definition: To store or stow in a safe place.
To enrich.
A syrupy byproduct of sugar refining; molasses or golden syrup.
Cloying sentimental speech.
Sweetheart (from treacle tart).
definitions.example: Listen, treacle, this is the last time I'll warn you!

definitions.definition: An antidote for poison; theriac.
Any all-powerful curative; a general remedy, a cure-all.
To apply treacle to a surface, so as to catch flies or moths, etc.
A young child who has started walking but not fully mastered it, typically between one and three years old.
An act of revenge.
definitions.example: They beat us last year, so this year's win was payback.

definitions.definition: A benefit, reward, a form of recompense.
A return on investment
A refund, reimbursement

An athletic discipline, in which practitioners traverse any environment in the most efficient way possible using their physical abilities, and which commonly involves running, jumping, vaulting, rolling, and other similar physical movements.
To freerun; to use parkour (to move over).
A person living on adjacent or nearby land; a person situated adjacently or nearby; anything (of the same type of thing as the subject) in an adjacent or nearby position.
definitions.example: My neighbour has two noisy cats.

definitions.definition: One who is near in sympathy or confidence.
A fellow human being.
To be adjacent to
definitions.example: Though France neighbours Germany, its culture is significantly different.

definitions.definition: (followed by "on"; figurative) To be similar to, to be almost the same as.
definitions.example: That sort of talk is neighbouring on treason.

definitions.definition: To associate intimately with; to be close to.
Exciting wonder or surprise; astonishing; wonderful; delightful.
definitions.example: I went to a marvelous party last week.

definitions.definition: A toy made of a soft material, usually stuffed with cotton, straw, beans, or other materials, and typically intended as a plaything for children.
Poorly ventilated; partially plugged.
definitions.example: I can’t smell very well today – I have a stuffy nose.

definitions.definition: Stout; mettlesome; resolute.
Angry and obstinate; sulky.
Boring, uninteresting, over-formal, pompous, very conventional.
definitions.example: The stuffy professor droned on as the class lost interest.

definitions.definition: Stout; sturdy.

Any plant of the genus Rheum, especially Rheum rhabarbarum, having large leaves and long green or reddish acidic leafstalks that are edible, in particular when cooked (although the leaves are mildly poisonous).

The leafstalks of common rhubarb or garden rhubarb (usually known as Rheum × hybridum), which are long, fleshy, often pale red, and with a tart taste, used as a food ingredient; they are frequently stewed with sugar and made into jam or used in crumbles, pies, etc.
The dried rhizome and roots of Rheum palmatum (Chinese rhubarb) or Rheum officinale (Tibetan rhubarb), from China, used as a laxative and purgative.
A Royal Air Force World War II code name for operations by aircraft (fighters and fighter-bombers) involving low-level flight to seek opportunistic targets.
Of fighter aircraft: to fire at a target opportunistically.
Of the colour of rhubarb: either brownish-yellow (the colour of rhubarb rhizomes and roots used for medicinal purposes), or pale red (often the colour of the leafstalks of common rhubarb).
An incident, action, or time period standing out by itself, but more or less connected with a complete series of events.
definitions.example: It was a most embarrassing episode in my life.

definitions.definition: An instalment of a drama told in parts, as in a TV series.
definitions.example: I can't wait till next week’s episode.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) A serving of beer.
Foamy or churned to the point of becoming infused with bubbles.
definitions.example: I like my milkshakes frothy, not flat like this!

definitions.definition: Lightweight; lacking depth or substance
definitions.example: a frothy argument

definitions.definition: A single aspect of a given thing.
A measure of spatial extent in a particular direction, such as height, width or breadth, or depth.
A construct whereby objects or individuals can be distinguished.
The number of independent coordinates needed to specify uniquely the location of a point in a space; also, any of such independent coordinates.
The number of elements of any basis of a vector space.
One of the physical properties that are regarded as fundamental measures of a physical quantity, such as mass, length and time.
definitions.example: The dimension of velocity is length divided by time.

definitions.definition: Any of the independent ranges of indices in a multidimensional array.
A universe or plane of existence.
To mark, cut or shape something to specified dimensions.
A curved, pointed horny nail on each digit of the foot of a mammal, reptile, or bird.
A foot equipped with such.
The pincer (chela) of a crustacean or other arthropod.
A mechanical device resembling a claw, used for gripping or lifting.
A slender appendage or process, formed like a claw, such as the base of petals of the pink.
The act of catching a ball overhand.
To scratch or to tear at.
To use the claws to seize, to grip.
To use the claws to climb.
To perform a claw catch.
To move with one's fingertips.
To relieve uneasy feeling, such as an itch, by scratching; hence, to humor or flatter, to court someone.
To rail at; to scold.
To flatter; to fawn on (a person).
Having a paunch; having a prominent stomach; potbellied.
definitions.example: He exercised every day to avoid becoming paunchy in his old age.

definitions.definition: A young cat, especially before sexual maturity (reached at about seven months).
A young rabbit, rat, hedgehog, squirrel, fox, beaver, badger, etc.
A moth of the genus Furcula.
A term of endearment, especially for a woman.
To give birth to kittens.
The left side of a horse or of a team of horses pulling a carriage etc.
To come closer to; to approach.
definitions.example: The ship nears the land.

definitions.definition: Physically close.
definitions.example: I can't see near objects very clearly without my glasses.

definitions.definition: Close in time.
definitions.example: The end is near.

definitions.definition: Closely connected or related.
definitions.example: The deceased man had no near relatives.

definitions.definition: Close to one's interests, affection, etc.; intimate; dear.
definitions.example: a near friend

definitions.definition: Close to anything followed or imitated; not free, loose, or rambling.
definitions.example: a version near to the original

definitions.definition: So as barely to avoid or pass injury or loss; close; narrow.
definitions.example: a near escape

definitions.definition: Approximate, almost.
definitions.example: The two words are near synonyms.

definitions.definition: (in relation to a vehicle) On the side nearest to the kerb (the left-hand side if one drives on the left).
definitions.example: The near front wheel came loose.

definitions.definition: Next to the driver, when he is on foot; on the left of an animal or a team.
definitions.example: the near ox; the near leg

definitions.definition: Immediate; direct; close; short.
Stingy; parsimonious.
At or towards a position close in space or time.
Nearly; almost.
definitions.example: He was near unconscious when I found him.

definitions.definition: Physically close to, in close proximity to.
definitions.example: There are habitable planets orbiting many of the stars near our Sun.

definitions.definition: Close to in time.
definitions.example: The voyage was near completion.

definitions.definition: Close to in nature or degree.
definitions.example: His opinions are near the limit of what is acceptable.

definitions.definition: A strip of cloth worn around the neck and tied in the front. See also bowtie.
A person who is actively engaged in battle, conflict or warfare; a soldier or combatant.
A person who is aggressively, courageously, or energetically involved in an activity, such as athletics.
(sometimes humorous) Look.
The release of an arrow.
A state of laxity or indulgence; unrestrained freedom, abandonment.
All play other than set pieces (scrums and line-outs).
Freedom from restraint.
A letting go; discharge.
To let loose, to free from restraints.
To unfasten, to loosen.
To make less tight, to loosen.
Of a grip or hold, to let go.
To shoot (an arrow)
To set sail.
To solve; to interpret.
Not fixed in place tightly or firmly.
definitions.example: This wheelbarrow has a loose wheel.

definitions.definition: Not held or packaged together.
definitions.example: 'You can buy apples in a pack, but they are cheaper loose.

definitions.definition: Not under control.
definitions.example: The dog is loose again.

definitions.definition: Not fitting closely
definitions.example: 'I wear loose clothes when it is hot.

definitions.definition: Not compact.
definitions.example: 'It is difficult walking on loose gravel.

definitions.definition: Relaxed.
definitions.example: She danced with a loose flowing movement.

definitions.definition: Not precise or exact; vague; indeterminate.
definitions.example: a loose way of reasoning

definitions.definition: Indiscreet.
definitions.example: Loose talk costs lives.

definitions.definition: (somewhat dated) Free from moral restraint; immoral, unchaste.
Not being in the possession of any competing team during a game.
definitions.example: He caught an elbow going after a loose ball.

definitions.definition: Not costive; having lax bowels.
Begin shooting; release your arrows
To move an object slowly and carefully in a particular direction.
definitions.example: He edged the book across the table.

definitions.definition: To move slowly and carefully in a particular direction.
definitions.example: He edged away from her.

definitions.definition: (usually in the form 'just edge') To win by a small margin.
To hit the ball with an edge of the bat, causing a fine deflection.
To trim the margin of a lawn where the grass meets the sidewalk, usually with an electric or gas-powered lawn edger.
To furnish with an edge; to construct an edging.
To furnish with an edge, as a tool or weapon; to sharpen.
To make sharp or keen; to incite; to exasperate; to goad; to urge or egg on.
To delay one's orgasm so as to remain almost at the point of orgasm.
Something that forms, defines or marks the edge.
definitions.example: The decorative edging around the door makes it easier to find in the dark.

definitions.definition: Technique involving the placement of the edge of the climbing shoe on a sharp hold.
The operation of shaping or dressing the edge of anything, as of a piece of metal.
The sexual technique of maintaining a high level of sexual arousal for an extended period of time without reaching orgasm.
A plant of the orchid family (Orchidaceae), bearing unusually-shaped flowers of beautiful colours.
A light bluish-red, violet-red or purple colour.
(colour) having a light purple colour.
The keeper of a personal journal, who writes in it regularly.
One whose occupation is journalism, originally only writing in the printed press.
A reporter, who professionally does living reporting on news and current events.
A woman who tends free-range cattle, especially in the American West.
A woman who identifies with cowboy culture, including clothing such as the cowboy hat.
A playing card of queen rank.
A sex position where the woman is on top; cowgirl position.
Any of various rod-shaped, spore-forming aerobic bacteria in the genus Bacillus, some of which cause disease.
Any bacilliform (rod-shaped) bacterium.
(by extension) Something which spreads like bacterial infection.
An inhabitant of the northern regions.
The northern pike.
Of, facing, situated in, or related to the north;northerly.
(of a wind) Blowing from the north; northerly.
Characteristic of the North of England (usually capitalised)
definitions.example: Les Dawson was a famous northern comedian.

definitions.definition: A piece of material that attracts some metals by magnetism.
(preceded by a noun) A person or thing that attracts what is denoted by the preceding noun.
definitions.example: He always had a girl on his arm – he's a bit of a babe magnet.

definitions.definition: The sound of laughing, produced by air so expelled; any similar sound.
definitions.example: Their loud laughter betrayed their presence.

definitions.definition: A movement (usually involuntary) of the muscles of the laughing face, particularly of the lips, and of the whole body, with a peculiar expression of the eyes, indicating merriment, satisfaction or derision, and usually attended by a sonorous and interrupted expulsion of air from the lungs.
A reason for merriment.
A group or association of cooperating members.
definitions.example: the League of Nations

definitions.definition: An organization of sports teams which play against one another for a championship.
definitions.example: My favorite sports organizations are the National Football League and the American League in baseball.

definitions.definition: (often in the negative) A class or type of people or things that are evenly matched or on the same level.
definitions.example: Forget about dating him; he's out of your league.

definitions.definition: A prefecture-level administrative unit in Inner Mongolia (Chinese: 盟).
To form an association; to unite in a league or confederacy; to combine for mutual support.
A version of rugby football, played between two teams of thirteen.
To remove a cover from.
definitions.example: The model railway was uncovered.

definitions.definition: To reveal the identity of.
definitions.example: The murderer has finally been uncovered.

definitions.definition: To show openly; to disclose; to reveal.
To remove one's hat or cap as a mark of respect.
To expose the genitalia.
To expose (lines of formation of troops) successively by the wheeling to right or left of the lines in front.
The act of judging.
The power or faculty of performing such operations; especially, when unqualified, the faculty of judging or deciding rightly, justly, or wisely
definitions.example: a man of judgment

definitions.definition: The conclusion or result of judging; an opinion; a decision.
The act of determining, as in courts of law, what is conformable to law and justice; also, the determination, decision, or sentence of a court, or of a judge.
The final award; the last sentence.
A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.
definitions.example: This is a strange kind of tobacco.

definitions.definition: A makeshift or otherwise atypical specimen.
definitions.example: The opening served as a kind of window.

definitions.definition: One's inherent nature; character, natural disposition.
Family, lineage.
Manner.
Goods or services used as payment, as e.g. in barter.
Equivalent means used as response to an action.
definitions.example: I'll pay in kind for his insult.

definitions.definition: Each of the two elements of the communion service, bread and wine.
